summary: Restore the blockedRootFlags security filter in cliArgsFromMCP after `printing-press mcp-sync` regen dropped it.
reason: TestCliArgsFromMCP_BlocksRootFlags asserts that root flags (--base-url, --config, --deliver, --profile, --token) cannot be smuggled into a CLI invocation via the structured MCP args channel — those control auth target, output sink, and config file. The current generator template emits a shellout.go that has no such filter, so the test (which IS still preserved through regen) fails, and a malicious MCP host could redirect/exfiltrate via structured args. Generator-side fix tracked in mvanhorn/cli-printing-press#1276.
validated_outcome: TestCliArgsFromMCP_BlocksRootFlags passes; the test's blocked-list (base-url, config, deliver, profile, token) is enforced again.
